channel.title: Infiniti Tech Partners — Insights
channel.description: Engineering, cloud, security and AI insights for growth-stage CTOs in the US and UK.

item.title: PCI DSS for SaaS: What You Actually Need to Handle Card Payments
item.description: What PCI DSS actually requires of a SaaS company taking card payments — how to slash your scope, which SAQ applies, the merchant levels, and the obligations you keep even with Stripe.

item.title: Scaling PostgreSQL: When to Tune, When to Replicate, When to Shard
item.description: A practical order of operations for scaling PostgreSQL under a growing SaaS — indexing and tuning, connection pooling, read replicas, partitioning, and when sharding is finally worth it.

item.title: Observability for Growth-Stage SaaS: Logs, Metrics, Traces, and On-Call
item.description: How to build observability for a growth-stage SaaS — the three pillars, what to instrument first, SLO-based alerting, and an on-call rotation that doesn't burn your team out.

item.title: The EU AI Act for US and UK SaaS: A Practical Compliance Guide
item.description: What the EU AI Act actually requires of US and UK SaaS companies that ship AI features — the risk tiers, the obligations that apply to most products, and the timeline.

item.title: Securing LLM Applications: Prompt Injection and the OWASP LLM Top 10
item.description: How to secure a production LLM application against prompt injection, data leakage, and the OWASP LLM Top 10 — the threat model, the defenses that work, and the ones that don't.

item.title: Disaster Recovery for SaaS: Setting RTO/RPO You Can Actually Hit
item.description: How to build a disaster recovery plan for SaaS around honest RTO and RPO targets — backups you've actually restored, tested failover, and a runbook that works at 3am.

item.title: LLM Cost Control: Cut Your AI Bill Without Cutting Quality
item.description: The levers that bring a runaway LLM bill under control — model routing, prompt caching, context discipline, and output limits — without users noticing a drop in quality.

item.title: GDPR for US SaaS Selling into the UK & EU: A Practical Guide
item.description: What a US SaaS company actually has to do to sell into the UK and EU under GDPR — lawful basis, data transfers, DPAs, subject rights, and the engineering work behind compliance.

item.title: Zero-Downtime Database Migrations: No Maintenance Window Required
item.description: How to ship schema changes to a live production database with zero downtime — using the expand-and-contract pattern, backfills, and dual writes instead of a 2am maintenance window.

item.title: Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture: Pooled, Siloed, or Bridge
item.description: The three multi-tenancy models for SaaS — pooled, siloed, and bridge — and how to pick the one that fits your security, cost, and scaling needs without painting yourself into a corner.

item.title: Monolith to Microservices Without Freezing the Roadmap
item.description: How to migrate a monolith to microservices incrementally — using the strangler pattern — without a risky big-bang rewrite or a multi-quarter feature freeze that stalls the business.

item.title: Edge Computing for SaaS: When the Latency Is Worth the Complexity
item.description: Edge computing promises lower latency and resilience, but adds real operational cost. A pragmatic 2026 guide for SaaS CTOs on when the edge pays off — and when a good CDN is enough.

item.title: ISO 27001 vs SOC 2: Which Compliance Path for US & UK SaaS in 2026
item.description: SOC 2 or ISO 27001 — or both? A practical 2026 guide for US and UK SaaS founders on which security certification your buyers actually want, what each costs, and how to sequence them.

item.title: Building a Private AI Assistant on Your Own Data: Architecture & Pitfalls
item.description: How to build a private, secure AI assistant grounded in your company's own data — the retrieval architecture, access control, and the pitfalls that sink most internal LLM projects.

item.title: Technical Due Diligence for a Series B Raise: The CTO's Checklist
item.description: What investors' technical diligence teams actually inspect before a Series B — architecture, security, delivery, and team risk — and how a growth-stage CTO gets ahead of it.

item.title: AI Agents in Production: Lessons from 10 Enterprise Rollouts
item.description: What separates AI agent projects that reach production from the ones that stall in demo — evaluation, scope, guardrails, and human oversight, drawn from real enterprise rollouts.

item.title: HIPAA-Compliant Software Architecture: Patterns That Hold Up
item.description: The architecture patterns that make healthtech software genuinely HIPAA-compliant — encryption, access control, audit logging, and PHI isolation that survive an audit.

item.title: Kubernetes or Serverless? The 2026 Decision Tree for Growth-Stage CTOs
item.description: When to run Kubernetes, when serverless cuts cost, and how most growth-stage companies use both — with a four-factor decision framework and cost benchmarks.

item.title: RAG vs Fine-Tuning vs Agents: Choosing the Right LLM Pattern in 2026
item.description: How to choose between RAG, fine-tuning, and agents for an enterprise AI system — with a decision tree, cost benchmarks, and the mistakes that cost teams 3–6 months.

item.title: What "Production-Ready" Actually Means (and Why Most MVPs Aren't)
item.description: A concrete definition of production-ready software — the checklist a growth-stage team should clear before calling an MVP done, and where most fall short.

item.title: AWS Cost Optimization: 12 Levers for SaaS at $5M–$50M ARR
item.description: The 12 levers that cut a growth-stage SaaS company's AWS bill by 30–50% — from rightsizing and savings plans to data-transfer and architecture fixes — without slowing the team down.

item.title: The Real Cost of an In-House Engineering Team: US vs UK
item.description: What a 6-person engineering team actually costs in New York and London once you add recruiting, ramp-up, management and attrition — and when that spend is worth it.

item.title: The 2026 Build vs Buy Decision Framework for Growth-Stage CTOs
item.description: A practical framework for deciding when to build custom software, when to buy off-the-shelf, and when to do both — with the hidden costs most teams miss on each side.

item.title: How to Hire an AI Consulting Partner: A Buyer's Guide for 2026
item.description: What to look for, what to ask, and the red flags to avoid when choosing an enterprise AI consulting partner — from a team that ships LLM and agent systems to production.

item.title: SOC 2 in 90 Days: The Engineering-Led Playbook
item.description: How a senior engineering team can take a growth-stage SaaS company from zero SOC 2 controls to a Type I attestation in 90 days — without buying a compliance platform.

item.title: Fractional Engineering Teams: A CTO's Guide for 2026
item.description: When growth-stage CTOs in the US and UK choose a fractional engineering team over hiring, what they actually gain — and what tradeoffs to negotiate up front.
